question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: validDurationSeconds] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a negative validDurationSeconds, causing the mirror node to store a transaction record with an invalid validity window that breaks downstream validity period calculations and misrepresents the transaction's temporal constraints in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: transactionHash] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a transactionHash that is identical to an existing transaction's hash but with a different consensusTimestamp, causing the transaction_hash table to have two records pointing to different consensus timestamps for the same hash, corrupting the one-to-one mapping between hashes and transactions in Hashgraph history?
